results.answer:
To hold your mail while on vacation, you can submit a USPS Hold Mail® request in two ways:

**Online:**
- Visit [USPS Hold Mail Service](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) 
- You'll need to sign in to or create a USPS.com® account
- You can submit requests up to 30 days in advance or as early as the next delivery day
- Requests before 2:00 A.M. Central Time can start the same business day; after 2:00 A.M. start the next business day

**In Person:**
- Visit your local Post Office and complete PS Form 8076 (Authorization to Hold Mail)
- You can also call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

Important details:
- The service is **free**
- It holds **ALL mail** (letters and packages) for **ALL individuals** at your address
- Only one Hold Mail service can be active per address at a time
- Save your confirmation number for future reference
- Postal business days are Monday-Saturday (excluding holidays)

Based on the information provided, there are two ways to change your address with USPS:

1. **Online**: Visit usps.com or Moversguide.USPS.com to complete your USPS Change of Address online

The form allows you to specify the type of change (individual, entire family, or business) and whether the forwarding is temporary or permanent.

For help with Informed Delivery Multifactor Authentication, please see [Multifactor Authentication (MFA)](https://faq.usps.com/s/article/Multifactor-Authentication-MFA) | **Overview** **What is Informed Delivery®?** Informed Delivery is a free and optional online notification feature that gives residential, business and eligible PO Box™ consumers the ability to digitally preview their letter-sized mail and manage their packages scheduled to arrive soon. The United States Postal Service® (USPS) digitally images the front of letter-sized mail that runs through our automated mail sorting equipment. USPS uses those images to provide digital notifications in advance of the delivery of physical mail. **Who is eligible for Informed Delivery®?** Informed Delivery is available to most residential and PO Box™ addresses, and many business addresses. Visit [informeddelivery.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) to check if your address is eligible and sign up. | | | --- | | **Note:** In rare instances, you may live in an eligible ZIP Code location, but can't sign up because your individual mailbox is not uniquely coded; this coding determines which images are provided to each Informed Delivery user and ensures that users can only view mail destined for their address. While most addresses are uniquely coded, not all are, especially in high density areas (e.g., apartment buildings or condos). If your address is not uniquely coded, you will not be able to sign up for Informed Delivery.
Visit [informeddelivery.usps.com](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm) to sign up and check if your address is eligible. | **What will Informed Delivery show me?** **Incoming Mailpieces:** You will receive daily emails to the email address associated with your [USPS.com](http://usps.com/) ® account containing images of the address side of up to 10 pieces of incoming letter mail. You can also view these images on your Informed Delivery online dashboard, where there is no limit on the number of images that will be shown. | | | --- | | **Note:** Some mailpieces (e.g., catalogs or magazines) are not imaged by our automated equipment and will not appear in your Informed Delivery email or dashboard. If a scan is detected on these types of mailpieces, you will see a message stating, "There is one or more mailpieces for which we do not currently have an image that is included in today's mail." | **Incoming and Outbound Packages:** You will also receive status updates for packages arriving at your address and packages you have sent at the bottom of your Informed Delivery Daily Digest email and within your Informed Delivery dashboard. **Informed Delivery Interactive Content:** Mailers and shippers can supplement their physical mailpiece or package with a custom image and call-to-action known as an interactive campaign. **4 Easy Steps for Domestic Labels:**

1. **Enter Label Information** - Include return and delivery addresses, package details, optional package value, and shipping date. Note: Domestic labels require a domestic return address (foreign return addresses are not allowed).

3. **Review and Pay** - Check your label information in the Shipping Cart, select your payment method, enter required information, and click "Next: Pay and Print."

4. **Print Your Label** - From the Payment Confirmation page, you can print immediately, save as PDF for later printing, or use the Label Broker® feature to print at a Post Office location.

**Additional Options:**
- Use the **Batch option** for multiple labels with the same weight, service, and return information
- Labels can be printed from Shipping History until 11:59 PM CST of the shipping date
- If you don't have a printer, use **Label Broker®** to get a QR code emailed to you for printing at any Post Office location

results.answer:
Yes, you can ship lithium batteries, but there are specific restrictions depending on whether you're shipping domestically or internationally, and how the batteries are packaged.

**For Domestic Shipments (including Alaska and Hawaii):**
- Batteries **contained in/installed in equipment**: Mailable by surface and air
- Batteries **packed with equipment but not installed**: Mailable by surface and air
- **Individual batteries** in original sealed packaging: Mailable by surface transport only, prohibited by air (except limited exceptions for intra-Alaska shipments)
- **Damaged, defective, or recalled batteries**: Prohibited unless approved by the director, Product Classification

**For International Shipments (including APO/FPO/DPO):**
- **Only new lithium batteries properly installed in electronic devices** are mailable
- **Prohibited**: New batteries packed with (but not installed in) equipment, individual batteries sent separately, and any used, damaged, defective, or recalled batteries

Different countries and military locations may have additional specific requirements, so you should check the Postal Explorer or International Mail Manual for your destination.

For complete packaging requirements and detailed regulations, refer to the "Lithium Batteries" entries on the USPS shipping restrictions pages.

To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these requirements:

**Service Required:**
- Use **Priority Mail Express®** service only (or Priority Mail Express International® for international shipments). No other service is permitted.

**Packaging Required:**
- Use the **USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE)** - this is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or artisan works.

**How to Get the Kit:**
- Order free kits from the Postal Store on USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- Order through Click-N-Ship® when creating a shipping label
- Check with your local Post Office (may be available behind the counter, but not always in stock)

**Additional Requirements:**
- Follow guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Priority Mail Express includes USPS Tracking®
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package

**International Shipping:**
- Permitted only when the destination country allows cremated remains and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country
- Check the Individual Country Listing in the International Mail Manual to verify

This applies to both human and animal cremated remains.

To hold your mail while on vacation, you can submit a USPS Hold Mail® request in two ways:

**Online:**
- Visit [USPS Hold Mail Service](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) 
- You'll need to sign in to or create a USPS.com® account
- You can submit requests up to 30 days in advance or as early as the next delivery day
- Requests before 2:00 A.M. Central Time can start the same business day; after 2:00 A.M. start the next business day

**In Person:**
- Visit your local Post Office and complete PS Form 8076 (Authorization to Hold Mail)
- You can also call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

Important details:
- The service is **free**
- It holds **ALL mail** (letters and packages) for **ALL individuals** at your address
- Only one Hold Mail service can be active per address at a time
- Save your confirmation number for future reference
- Postal business days are Monday-Saturday (excluding holidays)

- Batteries **packed with equipment but not installed**: Mailable by surface and air

- **Damaged, defective, or recalled batteries**: Prohibited unless approved by the director, Product Classification

**For International Shipments (including APO/FPO/DPO):**
- **Only new lithium batteries properly installed in electronic devices** are mailable
- **Prohibited**: New batteries packed with (but not installed in) equipment, individual batteries sent separately, and any used, damaged, defective, or recalled batteries

Different countries and military locations may have additional specific requirements, so you should check the Postal Explorer or International Mail Manual for your destination.

To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these requirements:

**Service Required:**
- Use **Priority Mail Express®** service only (or Priority Mail Express International® for international shipments). No other service is permitted.

**Packaging Required:**
- Use the **USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE)** - this is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or artisan works.

**How to Get the Kit:**
- Order free kits from the Postal Store on USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- Order through Click-N-Ship® when creating a shipping label
- Check with your local Post Office (may be available behind the counter, but not always in stock)

**Additional Requirements:**
- Follow guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Priority Mail Express includes USPS Tracking®
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package

**International Shipping:**
- Permitted only when the destination country allows cremated remains and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country
- Check the Individual Country Listing in the International Mail Manual to verify

This applies to both human and animal cremated remains.

To hold your mail while on vacation, you can submit a USPS Hold Mail® request in two ways:

**Online:**
- Visit [USPS Hold Mail Service](https://holdmail.usps.com/holdmail/) 
- You'll need to sign in to or create a USPS.com® account
- You can submit requests up to 30 days in advance or as early as the next delivery day
- Requests before 2:00 A.M. Central Time can start the same business day; after 2:00 A.M. start the next business day

**In Person:**
- Visit your local Post Office and complete PS Form 8076 (Authorization to Hold Mail)
- You can also call 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

Important details:
- The service is **free**
- It holds **ALL mail** (letters and packages) for **ALL individuals** at your address
- Only one Hold Mail service can be active per address at a time
- Save your confirmation number for future reference
- Postal business days are Monday-Saturday (excluding holidays)

results.answer:
Yes, you can ship lithium batteries, but there are specific restrictions depending on whether you're shipping domestically or internationally, and how the batteries are packaged.

**For Domestic Shipments (including Alaska and Hawaii):**
- Batteries **contained in/installed in equipment**: Mailable by surface and air
- Batteries **packed with equipment but not installed**: Mailable by surface and air
- **Individual batteries** in original sealed packaging: Mailable by surface transport only, prohibited by air (except limited exceptions for intra-Alaska shipments)
- **Damaged, defective, or recalled batteries**: Prohibited unless approved by the director, Product Classification

**For International Shipments (including APO/FPO/DPO):**
- **Only new lithium batteries properly installed in electronic devices** are mailable
- **Prohibited**: New batteries packed with (but not installed in) equipment, individual batteries sent separately, and any used, damaged, defective, or recalled batteries

Different countries and military locations may have additional specific requirements, so you should check the Postal Explorer or International Mail Manual for your destination.

For complete packaging requirements and detailed regulations, refer to the "Lithium Batteries" entries on the USPS shipping restrictions pages.

results.answer:
To mail cremated remains through USPS, you must follow these requirements:

**Service Required:**
- Use **Priority Mail Express®** service only (or Priority Mail Express International® for international shipments). No other service is permitted.

**Packaging Required:**
- Use the **USPS Priority Mail Express Cremated Remains box (BOX-CRE)** - this is required for all cremated remains shipments, including remains in jewelry, blown glass, or artisan works.

**How to Get the Kit:**
- Order free kits from the Postal Store on USPS.com (maximum 5 kits per order)
- Order through Click-N-Ship® when creating a shipping label
- Check with your local Post Office (may be available behind the counter, but not always in stock)

**Additional Requirements:**
- Follow guidelines in Publication 139, "How to Package and Ship Cremated Remains"
- Priority Mail Express includes USPS Tracking®
- Visit your local Post Office to ship the package

**International Shipping:**
- Permitted only when the destination country allows cremated remains and Priority Mail Express International service is available to that country
- Check the Individual Country Listing in the International Mail Manual to verify

This applies to both human and animal cremated remains.
